title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:1
body: Tā sīn mīm God knows best what He means by these letters.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:10
body: And mention O Muhammad (s) to your people when your Lord called to Moses on the night he saw the fire and the bush saying ‘Go to the wrongdoing folk as a messenger

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:100
body: So now we have no intercessors as the believers have in the way of angels prophets and other believers

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:101
body: nor any sympathetic friend who might be concerned for us.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:102
body: If only we had another turn another return to this world we would have been among those who believe!’ law ‘if only’ here is for the conditional optative to which nakūna ‘we would have been’ constitutes the response.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:103
body: Surely in that which is mentioned of the tale of Abraham and his people there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:104
body: And truly your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:105
body: The people of Noah denied the messengers by denying him for they all brought the same message affirming God’s Oneness; or it is because he stayed among them for so long that it was as though he was many messengers the feminine person of the verb kadhdhabat ‘denied’ governed by qawm ‘the people’ is on account of the import but it is masculine on account of its morphological form;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:106
body: when Noah their brother — their brother only in terms of lineage — said to them ‘Will you not fear? God?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:107
body: Truly I am a trusted messenger sent to you to deliver the Message with which I have been sent.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:108
body: So fear God and obey me in what I enjoin you to of God’s Oneness and obedience to Him.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:109
body: I do not ask of you any reward for it for delivering it; for my reward lies only with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:11
body: Pharaoh’s folk to them as well as him — they wronged their souls by disbelieving in God and wronged the Children of Israel by enslaving them — Will they not a-lā the hamza indicates a negation meant as a rebuke show fear?’ of God by being obedient to Him and affirm His Oneness?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:110
body: So fear God and obey me’ — he repeats this statement for emphasis.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:111
body: They said ‘Shall we believe in you in your words when it is the lowliest people who follow you?’ wa’ttaba‘aka a variant reading has wa-atbā‘uka ‘when your followers’ plural of tābi‘ ‘follower’ as a subject the riffraff such as the weavers and the shoemakers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:112
body: He said ‘And what do I know of what they may have been doing?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:113
body: Their reckoning is only my Lord’s concern and He will requite them if only you were aware — had you known this you would not have reviled them.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:114
body: And I am not about to drive away the believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:115
body: I am just a plain warner’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:116
body: They said ‘Lo! if you do not desist O Noah from what you say to us you will assuredly be among those assailed’ with stones or curses.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:117
body: He Noah said ‘My Lord my people have denied me

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:118
body: so judge conclusively between me and them and deliver me and the believers who are with me’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:119
body: He exalted be He says So We delivered him and those who were with him in the laden ark the ship filled with humans animals and birds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:12
body: He Moses said ‘My Lord I fear they will deny me

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:120
body: Then afterwards after We delivered them We drowned the rest of his people.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:121
body: Surely in that there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:122
body: Truly your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:123
body: ‘Ād denied the messengers

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:124
body: when Hūd their brother said to them ‘Will you not fear God?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:125
body: Truly I am a trusted messenger sent to you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:126
body: So fear God and obey me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:127
body: I do not ask of you any reward for this; for my reward lies only with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:128
body: Do you build on every prominence every high place a monument an edifice as a signpost for passers-by so that you may hurl abuse? at those who pass by you and deride them ta‘bathūna is a circumstantial qualifier referring to the subject of the verb tabnūna ‘you build’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:129
body: And you set up structures for extracting water underground that perhaps you might as though you will last forever therein and not die.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:13
body: and that anguish will constrain my breast because of their denial of me and that my tongue will not utter clearly to deliver the Message because of the knot in it. So give the Mission to my brother Aaron alongside me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:130
body: And when you assault to smite or slay you assault like tyrants mercilessly!

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:131
body: So fear God in this and obey me in what I have commanded you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:132
body: And fear Him Who has provided you Who has been gracious to you in a way that you know

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:133
body: provided you with cattle and sons

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:134
body: and gardens orchards and springs rivers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:135
body: Indeed I fear for you the chastisement of a tremendous day’ in this world and in the Hereafter should you disobey me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:136
body: They said ‘It is the same to us whether you admonish us or are not one of those who admonish anyway. In other words we will not heed your admonition.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:137
body: This that you threaten us with is merely the fabrication of the ancients that is their inventions and lies a variant reading for khalq ‘fabrication’ is khuluq ‘ethos’ in other words this denial of resurrection to which we adhere is merely ‘the ethos of the ancients’ in other words their nature and wont;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:138
body: and we will not be chastised’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:139
body: So they denied him concerning the chastisement whereupon We destroyed them in this world with the barren wind cf. Q. 5141. Surely in that there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:14
body: And I have sinned against them by my slaying of the Egyptian man and I fear they will slay me’ for this.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:140
body: Truly your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:141
body: Thamūd denied the messengers

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:142
body: when Sālih their brother said to them ‘Will you fear God?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:143
body: Truly I am a trusted messenger sent to you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:144
body: So fear God and obey me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:145
body: I do not ask of you any reward for this; for my reward lies only with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:146
body: Will you be left secure in that which is here of good things —

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:147
body: amid gardens and springs

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:148
body: and farms and date palms with slender delicate and tender spathes?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:149
body: And you hew dwellings out of the mountains arrogantly a variant reading of farihīna ‘arrogant’ has fārihīn ‘skilfully’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:15
body: Said He exalted be He ‘Certainly not! they shall not slay you. Go both of you you and your brother — this address although in the dual form is predominantly for the person present as opposed to the absent one — with Our signs. We will indeed be with you hearing what you say and what is said to you ma‘akum the two Moses and Aaron are referred to here with the plural pronoun.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:150
body: So fear God and obey me in what I have commanded you

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:151
body: and do not obey the command of the prodigal

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:152
body: who cause corruption in the earth through acts of disobedience and act righteously’ by being obedient to God.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:153
body: They said ‘You are indeed one of the bewitched those who have succumbed so many times to sorcery that their minds have been overcome.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:154
body: And moreover you are just a human being like us. So bring us a sign if you are sincere’ in your Mission.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:155
body: He said ‘This is a she-camel; she shall drink a share of the water and you shall drink each on a known day.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:156
body: And do not cause her any harm for then you shall be seized by the chastisement of an awful day’ awful because of the awfulness of the chastisement.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:157
body: But they hamstrung her — a group of them hamstrung her with the approval of others; and then became remorseful for having hamstrung it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:158
body: So they were seized by the chastisement which had been promised and were destroyed. Surely in that there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:159
body: Truly your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:16
body: So the two of you approach Pharaoh and say “Indeed We are — indeed each of the two of us is the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds to you

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:160
body: The people of Lot denied the messengers

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:161
body: when Lot their brother said to them ‘Will you not fear God?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:162
body: Truly I am a trusted messenger sent to you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:163
body: So fear God and obey me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:164
body: I do not ask of you any reward for this; my reward lies only with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:165
body: What! Of all people you come unto males to fornicate

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:166
body: and forsake the wives your Lord has created for you? in other words you forsake intimacy with their front parts? Nay but you are a transgressing folk’ overstepping the bounds of what is lawful into what is unlawful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:167
body: They said ‘If you do not desist O Lot from reproaching us you will assuredly be of those expelled’ from our land.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:168
body: He Lot said ‘Truly I abhor I detest what you do.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:169
body: My Lord deliver me and my family from what they do’ that is from the punishment for it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:17
body: that you should let go forth with us to Syria the Children of Israel” ’. So they approached him and said to him that which has been mentioned.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:170
body: So We delivered him and all his family

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:171
body: except an old woman — his wife — among those who stayed behind whom We destroyed.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:172
body: Then We destroyed all the others

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:173
body: and We rained on them a rain We rained on them stones — this detail pertains to the previous statement concerning their destruction — and evil was their rain the rain of those who were warned!

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:174
body: Surely in that there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:175
body: Surely your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:176
body: The dwellers in the wood a variant reading for ashābu’l-aykati has ashābu laykata a thicket of trees near Midian denied the messengers

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:177
body: when Shu‘ayb — He God does not say ‘their brother’ in this instance because he Shu‘ayb was not in terms of lineage one of them — said to them ‘Will you not fear God?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:178
body: Truly I am a trusted messenger sent to you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:179
body: So fear God and obey me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:18
body: He Pharaoh said to Moses ‘Did we not rear you among us in our homes as a child? as an infant only recently born but weaned and did you not stay with us for years of your life? for thirty years — he would dress from Pharaoh’s clothes and ride chariots of his and was referred to as Pharaoh’s son.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:180
body: I do not ask of you any reward for this; indeed my reward lies only with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:181
body: Give full measure and do not be of those who give short measure

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:182
body: and weigh with an even balance

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:183
body: and do not defraud people in their goods do not diminish anything of what is due to them and do not be degenerate in the earth seeking corruption by killing or otherwise ta‘thaw ‘degenerate’ derives from ‘athiya meaning afsada ‘to corrupt’; mufsidīna ‘seeking corruption’ is a circumstantial qualifier emphasising the import of its operator ta‘thaw.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:184
body: And fear Him Who created you and the former generations’ all creation before you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:185
body: They said ‘You are indeed one of the bewitched.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:186
body: You are just a human being like us. And we indeed in softened in place of the hardened form its subject omitted in other words it would be innahu think that you are one of the liars.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:187
body: Then make fragments read kisfan or kisafan of the heaven fall upon us if you are of the truthful’ in your Mission.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:188
body: He said ‘My Lord knows best what you are doing’ and will requite you for it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:189
body: But they denied him so they were seized by the chastisement of the day of the shade al-zulla — a cloud which gave them shade when they suffered a bout of extreme heat but which then rained down fire on them and they were incinerated. Assuredly it was the chastisement of a tremendous day.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:19
body: And you committed that deed of yours — namely his slaying of the Egyptian — being an ingrate’ a denier of my favours to you in having reared you among us and not enslaved you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:190
body: Surely in that there is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:191
body: Surely your Lord He is the Mighty the Merciful.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:192
body: Truly it the Qur’ān is the revelation of the Lord of the Worlds

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:193
body: brought down by the Trustworthy Spirit Gabriel

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:194
body: upon your heart that you may be one of the warners

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:195
body: in a clear Arabic tongue a variant reading for nazala ‘he brought down’ verse 193 has nuzzila ‘was sent down’ and al-rūha ‘the Spirit’ in the accusative the subject of the verb being God.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:196
body: And truly it the allusion to the Qur’ān that would be revealed to Muhammad (s) is in the Scriptures the Books of the ancients such as the Torah and the Gospel.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:197
body: Is it not a sign of this fact for them for the disbelievers of Mecca that the learned of the Children of Israel recognise it? those such as ‘Abd Allāh b. Salām and his companions from among those who became Muslim believers they are able inform you of this read yakun with āyatan in the accusative or takun with āyatun in the nominative.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:198
body: For had We revealed it to some non-Arabs a‘jamūn is the plural of a‘jam

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:199
body: and had he recited it to them that is to the disbelievers of Mecca they would not have believed in it disdaining to follow it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:2
body: Those namely these signs are the signs of the Manifest Book the Qur’ān the genitive annexation āyātu’l-kitābi conveys the partitive meaning of min the Manifest meaning the one that manifests what is truth from what is falsehood.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:20
body: He Moses said ‘I did that then when I was astray from the knowledge and the Message which God would later give me.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:200
body: So in the same way in which We would have caused denial of it to penetrate the hearts of disbelievers had it been recited by a non-Arab We have caused denial of it to penetrate the hearts of the criminals the Meccan disbelievers when Muhammad (s) recites it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:201
body: They will not believe in it until they behold the painful chastisement

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:202
body: so that it will come upon them suddenly while they are not aware.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:203
body: Thereupon they will say ‘Shall we be granted any respite?’ that we might become believers?; and it shall be said to them ‘No!’ and they will say ‘When will this chastisement be?’ God exalted be He says

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:204
body: So do they seek to hasten Our chastisement?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:205
body: Consider this then — inform Me If We were to let them enjoy life for many years

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:206
body: then there were to come on them that which they have been promised of chastisement

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:207
body: in what way mā here is interrogative would that enjoyment which they were given avail them? in averting the chastisement or mitigating it? It would be of no avail!

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:208
body: And We have not destroyed any town but it had warners messengers to warn its inhabitants;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:209
body: as a reminder an admonition for them; for We were never unjust for We destroy them only after they have been warned. The following was revealed to refute what the idolaters were saying

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:21
body: So I fled from you as I was afraid of you. Then my Lord gave me judgement knowledge and made me one of the messengers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:210
body: It the Qur’ān has not been brought down by the devils.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:211
body: Neither would it behove befit them to bring it down nor are they capable of doing that.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:212
body: Truly they are barred restrained with blazing stars from the hearing of the sayings of the angels.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:213
body: So do not invoke any other god with God lest you be among the chastised — if you were to do what they call you to.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:214
body: And warn the nearest of your kinsfolk namely the Banū Hāshim and the Banū al-Muttalib ‘He the Prophet warned them publicly …’ as reported by al-Bukhārī and Muslim.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:215
body: And lower your wing show your gentle side to the believers the affirmers of God’s Oneness who follow you.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:216
body: But if they your kinsfolk disobey you say to them ‘I am absolved of what you do’ in worshipping gods other than God.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:217
body: And put your trust wa-tawakkal or read fa-tawakkal ‘then put your trust’ in the Mighty the Merciful God in other words entrust Him with all your affairs

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:218
body: Who sees you when you stand for prayer

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:219
body: and your movements — in performing the various parts of prayer standing sitting bowing and prostrating — among those who prostrate those who perform the prayers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:22
body: That is a favour with which you now reproach me that you have enslaved the Children of Israel’ an ‘abbadta banī Isrā’īl is explicative of tilka ‘that’ in other words you have enslaved them but not me there is no favour in that for your enslavement of them is wrong some scholars have reckoned there to be an interrogative hamza of denial at the beginning of this verse a-tilka ni‘matun ‘is that a favour?’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:220
body: Truly He is the Hearing the Knowing.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:221
body: Shall I inform you O disbelievers of Mecca upon whom the devils descend? tanazzalu one of the tā’ letters in the original tatanazzalu has been omitted.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:222
body: They descend upon every sinful profligate liar such as Musaylama and other soothsayers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:223
body: They the devils report the heard sayings what they hear from the angels they report to the soothsayers but most of them are liars adding much that is false to what they hear — this was revealed before the devils were barred from the heaven.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:224
body: As for the poets only the perverse follow them in their poetry propounding it and reciting it on their behalf to others; they are thus reprehensible.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:225
body: Have you not noticed that — are you not aware that — in every valley of the valleys of rhetoric and the art thereof they rove overstepping the bounds of decency in their eulogies and diatribes

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:226
body: and that they say we do what they do not do? in other words that they lie.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:227
body: Save those poets who believe and perform righteous deeds and remember God frequently in other words those whom poetry does not distract from the remembrance of God and vindicate themselves by lampooning the disbelievers after they have been wronged by the disbelievers’ reviling of them as belonging together with all believers — such individuals are not reprehensible. God exalted be He says God does not like the utterance of evil words out loud unless a person has been wronged Q. 4148; He exalted be He also says so whoever commits aggression against you commit aggression against him in the manner that he committed against you Q. 2194; and those poets and others who are wrongdoers will soon know the reversal the return with which they will meet by which they will return after death.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:23
body: Pharaoh said to Moses ‘And what is “the Lord of the Worlds” ’ the One Whose Messenger you say you indeed are? In other words what exactly is He? And since there is no way for creation to know the true nature of God but only to know Him through an articulation of His attributes Moses blessings and peace be upon him responds to him with mention of some of these attributes

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:24
body: He said ‘The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in other words the Creator of all that — should you have conviction’ that He exalted be He is their Creator then believe in Him alone.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:25
body: He Pharaoh said to those noblemen among his folk who were around him ‘Did you not hear?!’ his response how it does not correspond to the question I asked?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:26
body: He Moses said ‘Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers’. Now even though this fact is comprised by what was said before it enrages Pharaoh and therefore

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:27
body: He said ‘Verily this messenger of yours sent to you is a madman!’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:28
body: He Moses said ‘The Lord of the east and the west and all that is between them — should you comprehend’ that He is such a Lord then believe in Him alone.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:29
body: He Pharaoh said to Moses ‘If you choose any god other than me I will surely make you a prisoner!’. His Pharaoh’s prison was very harsh for a person would be shut up in a dungeon underground alone where he could neither see nor hear anyone else.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:3
body: Perhaps O Muhammad (s) you might kill yourself out of distress for the reason that they namely the people of Mecca will not become believers la‘alla here expresses sympathy in other words what is meant is ‘have pity on it your self by alleviating the burden of this distress’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:30
body: He Moses said to him ‘What would you do this even if I bring you something manifest?’ patent proof of my Mission?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:31
body: He Pharaoh said to him ‘Then bring it if you are truthful’ in this.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:32
body: So he threw down his staff and lo! it was a manifest serpent a great snake.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:33
body: Then he drew out his hand he brought it out of his pocket and lo! it was white glowing before the onlookers and not its usual skin colour.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:34
body: He Pharaoh said to the council around him ‘Surely this man is a cunning sorcerer an expert in the art of magic

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:35
body: who seeks to expel you from your land by his sorcery. So what do you advise?’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:36
body: They said ‘Put him and his brother off for a while postpone judgement of their affair and send musterers into the cities

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:37
body: to bring you every cunning sorcerer’ to surpass Moses in the art of magic.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:38
body: So the sorcerers were assembled at a fixed time of a known day which was the morning of the Festival Day.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:39
body: And it was said to the people ‘Will you assemble!’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:4
body: If We will We will send down to them a sign from the heaven before which their necks will remain fa-zallat although grammatically in the past tense is being used in the sense of the imperfect tense bowed in humility and they will believe since ‘necks’ are described as ‘bowed in humility’ a status which in fact refers to those who possess them the adjectival form khādi‘ūn ‘bowed in humility’ used therein is the one normally used for rational beings.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:40
body: Maybe we will follow the sorcerers should they be the victors’ the interrogative of the previous verse is an incitement to assemble and the optative sense implicit in la‘alla is due to their anticipation of victory and hence their continued adherence to their religion and rejection of Moses.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:41
body: So when the sorcerers came they said to Pharaoh ‘Shall we indeed read a-inna either by pronouncing both hamzas or by not pronouncing the second but in both cases inserting an alif have a reward if we were to be the victors?’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:42
body: He said ‘Yes of course! And you shall then be among those closest to me’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:43
body: Moses said to them after they had said to him Either you cast or we shall be the casters Q. 7115 ‘Cast what you have to cast’ — the command to do this signifies an authorisation for them to cast and a means whereby the truth will be made to prevail.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:44
body: So they cast their ropes and their staffs and said ‘By the power of Pharaoh we shall surely be the victors!’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:45
body: Thereat Moses cast his staff and lo! it was swallowing talqafu one of the original two tā’ letters of taltaqifu has been assimilated what they had faked what they had transformed through illusion making their ropes and staffs appear to be moving serpents.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:46
body: So the sorcerers fell down prostrating.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:47
body: They said ‘We believe in the Lord of the Worlds

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:48
body: the Lord of Moses and Aaron’ for they realised that what they had witnessed in the case of the staff could not have been the result of mere sorcery.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:49
body: Pharaoh said ‘Do you believe a-āmantum read pronouncing both hamzas or replacing the second one with an alif him Moses though I have not given you leave? He is indeed your chief the one who has taught you sorcery — so that he only taught you some tricks and then surpassed you with others. Soon you will know what I will do with you! I will assuredly cut off your hands and legs on opposite sides — in other words the right hand of each one together with his left leg — then I shall surely crucify you all.’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:5
body: And there would never come to them from the Compassionate One any remembrance any revelation from the Qur’ān that is new muhdathin is an explicative adjective but that they used to disregard it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:50
body: They said ‘There is no harm in that no damage will be done to us thereby. Surely to our Lord after we die whichever way that may be we shall return in the Hereafter.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:51
body: Indeed We hope our Lord will forgive us our iniquities for being the first to believe’ in our time.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:52
body: And We revealed to Moses after many years which he spent among them calling them to the truth with God’s signs but all of which only increased them in insolence saying ‘Journey with My servants the Children of Israel by night a variant reading for an asri is an isr from the verb sarā one form of asrā go with them by night in the direction of the sea for indeed you will be pursued’. Pharaoh and his armies will pursue you but when they enter the sea in pursuit of you I will deliver you and drown them.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:53
body: Then Pharaoh having been informed of their night departure sent to the cities — it is said that he ruled a thousand cities and twelve thousand villages — musterers to assemble the army saying

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:54
body: ‘Lo! these are but a small gang. It is said that they the Children of Israel numbered 670000 while his Pharaoh’s vanguard alone numbered 700000 and so he deemed their number insignificant in comparison to his huge army.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:55
body: And indeed they have enraged us their actions infuriate us

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:56
body: and indeed we are all on our guard’ ready a variant reading for hadhirūna has hādhirūn meaning ‘alert’.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:57
body: God exalted be He says So We made them Pharaoh and his folk go forth out of Egypt in order to pursue Moses and his people from gardens orchards on either side of the Nile and springs streams running from the Nile through their dwellings

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:58
body: and treasures visible riches of gold and silver — these riches are called kunūz as opposed to amwāl ‘riches’ because that portion which is always due to God exalted be He from them had not been paid — and splendid places exquisite residences for princes and ministers surrounded by their retinues.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:59
body: So it was that is Our expulsion of them was as We have described and We made the Children of Israel to inherit these after drowning Pharaoh and his folk.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:6
body: Verily then they have denied it; but soon there will come to them the news the consequences of that which they used to deride.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:60
body: Then they pursued them they caught them up at sunrise.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:61
body: And when the two hosts sighted each other the companions of Moses said ‘We have been caught!’ Pharaoh’s hosts have caught us up and we have no power against them.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:62
body: He Moses said ‘Certainly not! they shall not catch us up; indeed I have my Lord with me to provide assistance. He will guide me’ to the route of deliverance.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:63
body: God exalted be He says Thereupon We revealed to Moses ‘Strike the sea with your staff’ and he struck it whereupon it parted it split into twelve parts and each part was as a mighty mountain between each of these parts were paths which they followed such that not even the saddles or the felts of the riders were touched by water.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:64
body: And there We brought near the others Pharaoh and his folk until they followed them across the same paths.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:65
body: And We delivered Moses and all those who were with him by bringing them out of the sea while it was in the state mentioned above.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:66
body: Then We drowned the others Pharaoh and his folk by closing the sea on them after they had entered the sea and the Children of Israel had exited therefrom.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:67
body: Surely in that drowning of Pharaoh and his folk there is a sign a lesson for those generations who would come after them; but most of them are not believers in God. The only ones to believe were Āsiya — Pharaoh’s wife — Hizqīl the believing man of Pharaoh’s family and Maryam daughter of Nāmūsī she who pointed out the bone remains of the prophet Joseph peace be upon him.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:68
body: And surely your Lord He is the Mighty for He wrought vengeance upon the disbelievers by drowning them the Merciful to believers delivering them from drowning.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:69
body: And recite to them the disbelievers of Mecca the tiding the tale of Abraham Ibrāhīma is substituted by the following idh qāla ‘when he said’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:7
body: Have they not contemplated observed the earth how many We have caused to grow therein in other words We have caused to grow therein many of every splendid kind of vegetation of every fair species.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:70
body: when he said to his father and his people ‘What do you worship?’

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:71
body: They said ‘We worship idols — they reiterate the verb in the response in order to supplement it with the following remark — and remain cleaving to them’ we remain worshipping them all day; they give a longer response to indicate their misplaced pride in such an act.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:72
body: He said ‘Do they hear you when you supplicate?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:73
body: Or do they bring you benefit when you worship them or harm? to you if you do not?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:74
body: They said ‘Rather we found our fathers so doing’ that is doing the same as we do.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:75
body: He said ‘Have you considered what you have been worshipping

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:76
body: you and your ancestors?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:77
body: They are indeed hateful to me I do not worship them; but the Lord of the Worlds I do worship Him

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:78
body: He Who created me it is He Who guides me to the proper religion

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:79
body: and provides me with food and drink

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:8
body: Surely in that there is a sign an indication of the perfection of His power exalted be He; but most of them in God’s knowledge are not believers kāna here according to Sībawayhi is extra.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:80
body: and when I am sick it is He Who cures me;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:81
body: and Who will make me die then give me life

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:82
body: and Who I hope will forgive me my iniquity on the Day of Judgement the Day of Requital.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:83
body: My Lord! Grant me unerring judgement knowledge and unite me with the righteous the prophets.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:84
body: And confer on me a worthy repute excellent praise among posterity those who will come after me up to the Day of Resurrection.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:85
body: And make me among the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss among those who will be given it.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:86
body: And forgive my father for indeed he is one of those who are astray by Your relenting to him and forgiving him this was before it became clear to him Abraham that he his father was an enemy of God as mentioned in sūrat Barā’a Q. 9114.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:87
body: And do not disgrace me do not expose me on the day when they mankind are resurrected;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:88
body: of which day God exalted be He says the following the day when neither wealth nor children will avail anyone

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:89
body: except him who comes to God with a heart that is sound free from idolatry and hypocrisy — and this is the heart of the believer whom such things will avail.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:9
body: And surely your Lord He is the Mighty One Who will exact vengeance from the disbelievers the Merciful Who shows mercy to believers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:90
body: And Paradise will be brought near for the God-fearing and they will be able to see it

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:91
body: and Hell will be revealed plainly for the perverse the disbelievers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:92
body: And it will be said to them ‘Where is that which you used to worship

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:93
body: besides God? that is other than Him in the way of idols. Do they help you by averting from you the chastisement or do they help one another?’ by averting it from themselves? No neither!

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:94
body: Then they will be hurled cast into it they and the perverse

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:95
body: and the hosts of Iblīs his followers and those jinn and humans who were obedient to him all together.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:96
body: They the perverse shall say as they wrangle therein with those whom they used to worship

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:97
body: ‘By God we had indeed in softened in place of the hardened form with its subject omitted in other words it would usually be innahu been in manifest error

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:98
body: when we equated you in worship with the Lord of the Worlds.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 26:99
body: And it was none other than the sinners the devils or our predecessors whose example we were following that led us astray from guidance.
